legend.covered: already proven green by an existing real-component test (cited); NOT rewritten this round
legend.covered-new: the smallest missing negative, added this round in the owned lane
legend.residual: a gap that is out-of-lane to fix (would widen Ring 0 / supervisor / authority) — reported, not changed

proof_kind_note: Every row below is COMPONENT proof (real modules, disposable repos, deterministic transports). A live HTTP wire transcript over loopback (approve/bind door + mind door on a real socket, real headers) is a SEPARATE, still-required artifact and is NOT claimed here — see live_wire_required.

rows.invariant: the council/reviewer is advisory (grantsAuthority:false); a well-formed 'pass' is necessary-but-not-sufficient and can never substitute for the owner signature, so misleading evidence cannot cause an effect

rows.threat: Fu advice says approve while grantsAuthority:false
rows.invariant: advisory-pass authorizes nothing; only a valid hybrid AUMLOK signature authorizes; the two gates are separate

rows.threat: forged AUMLOK material (ML-DSA-65 half — both halves load-bearing)
byte / content mismatch (same intent, different bytes)
wrong-PURPOSE AUMLOK material (ML-DSA half bound to a different purpose domain)
rows.invariant: the ML-DSA-65 half is purpose-domain-bound; a signature valid for another purpose (or a made-up purpose) cannot authorize an owner promotion — isolated against a positive control that differs ONLY in the context
rows.kernel_adjacent: packages/kernel/test/evidence.test.ts:62-70 proves the mirror case for the receipt-head path; this proves the owner-promotion path used by the seed lane

rows.threat: prompt/content injection attempting to cross into authority
rows.invariant: authority-shaped / secret-shaped content is refused at the shape gate and never reaches a trace or receipt; content never enters an authorization predicate

rows.threat: protected gate/supervisor/signer targets (incl. WAVE 2 ceremony membrane)
rows.invariant: the frozen SELF_PROTECTING list refuses candidate materialization of the fence/gate/monitor/doors/kernel-authority/scanners AND the WAVE 2 membrane, table-independently

rows.threat: crash after receipt but before effect
rows.invariant: restart never auto-resumes an effect; re-running over the same durable state materializes nothing unless explicitly asked; a re-attempt against an existing candidate branch/worktree refuses rather than double-applies

rows.threat: crash after effect but before completion receipt
rows.invariant: restart reconciles as applied-exactly-once; a different proposal reusing a consumed nonce is a genuine replay refusal, not a reconciliation

rows.threat: candidate symlink / path-identity / unrelated-file / staged-set attacks (WAVE 3)
corrupt / stale Convex projection trying to become a second truth
rows.invariant: projections are non-authoritative; the gate re-verifies authority in-process from scratch; a tampered ownerVerified:true or an extra/hidden projection key is rejected; a corrupt store fails closed, burns no nonce, stays retryable

residuals.note: The workflow supervisor state modules (durableRecursion.ts / metabolism.ts / eventStream.ts) are NOT on the frozen SELF_PROTECTING list. They hold NO authority (projections-only; authority stays in-process), so targeting them as a candidate is Ring-1 substrate, not a Ring-0 authority bypass. Adding them would WIDEN the self-protecting surface — out of lane for R44 (which must not widen Ring 0 / supervisor / authority). Reported for an owner ruling: should the supervisor substrate be candidate-fenced as well?

LIVE HTTP WIRE TRANSCRIPT is still required and NOT provided here. Every row above is component proof (function-level, disposable repos, injected transports). The approve/bind door + mind door have not been exercised over a real loopback socket with real Origin/Host/Sec-Fetch headers end-to-end. That transcript is Sam 1's headless-proof deliverable; this matrix is its component-level precondition, not a substitute.
